back.text: A flashcard app for your Apple Watch. Study any subject in quick sessions right on your wrist — no phone needed once a deck is installed.
Read the question, think of your answer, then tap the card to flip it and reveal the answer. Check yourself, then move on.
back.markdown:
## Think, then tap 👆

1. Read the **question**
2. Recall the answer in your head
3. **Tap** to flip and reveal it

{#0F766E}Trying to remember *before* you flip is what makes it stick.{/}

front.text: How do I say if I got a card right or wrong?
front.markdown: After flipping, how do I mark a card **right or wrong**?

back.text: After the answer is shown, swipe right if you got it correct, or swipe left if you missed it. Your honesty trains what you see next.
back.markdown:
## Swipe to grade ↔️

- Swipe **right** → you got it ✅
- Swipe **left** → you missed it ❌

{#0F766E}Be honest — your grades decide which cards come back sooner.{/}

front.text: Why does a question come back later?
front.markdown: Why do some questions keep **coming back**?

back.text: WristRecall uses spaced repetition. Cards you find hard return sooner; cards you know well are spaced further apart, so you spend time where it counts.
A deck is a collection of cards on one subject — like Spanish verbs or world capitals. You pick a deck, then study its cards.

front.text: How do I get more decks?

back.text: Find decks on the WristRecall decks site or GitHub repo, download a .wristdeck file, then open it to import the deck into the app.
back.markdown:
## Open a .wristdeck file 📥

1. Find decks on the WristRecall **decks site** or **GitHub** repo
2. Download the **`.wristdeck`** file
3. **Open** it to import the deck

{#0F766E}There's no in-app store — you add decks by opening a file you download.{/}

front.text: How do I study on my Apple Watch?
front.markdown: How do I get a deck onto my **Apple Watch**?

back.text: In the iPhone Deck Browser, swipe left on a deck and tap Install on Watch. It syncs over to your watch so you can study anywhere — no phone required.
back.markdown:
## Install on Watch ⌚️

1. Open the **Deck Browser** on iPhone
2. **Swipe left** on a deck
3. Tap **Install on Watch**

{#0F766E}It syncs automatically — then study with your phone in your pocket.{/}

front.text: How long should a study session be?
front.markdown: How long should a **study session** be?

back.text: Short and often beats long and rare. A minute or two on your wrist, a few times a day, adds up fast.

front.text: How do I build a daily habit?
front.markdown: How do I build a **daily habit**?

back.text: Add the WristRecall widget or complication to your watch face for a one-tap start, and keep your streak going by studying a little every day.
back.markdown:
## Make it one tap 🔥

- Add the **complication** to your watch face
- Or the **widget** on your home screen

{#0F766E}Study a little every day to keep your streak alive.{/}

front.text: Where can I get help or track progress?
front.markdown: Where do I **track progress** or get help?

back.text: Check your accuracy and streak stats in the app after each session. For tips and support, visit the WristRecall help pages. Now pick a deck and start learning!
back.markdown:
## You're ready! 🎉

- See your **accuracy** and **streak** in Stats
- Find tips on the **Help** pages

{#0F766E}Now pick a deck and start learning — one card at a time.{/}
